# UX Checklist: 007 — Dashboard Design

**Purpose**: Validate whether the dashboard requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable enough for implementation and review.
**Created**: 2026-04-01
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are loading, empty, and error-state requirements explicitly defined for both Section A cards with distinct triggers and user-facing copy? [Completeness, Spec §Section A]
- [x] CHK002 Are the required data fields for the Course Information Card fully specified, including deterministic tie-breaking when multiple courses qualify as “next”? [Completeness, Spec §Section A]
- [x] CHK003 Are the Slide Status requirements complete for all outcomes of `fetchSlideStatus()` including pending, populated, empty, and I/O failure states? [Completeness, Spec §Section A, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK004 Are all four dashboard sections defined with enough structural detail to determine what belongs in each section without inferring hidden layout rules? [Completeness, Spec §Dashboard Composition]
- [x] CHK005 Are wrapper-versus-owner responsibilities fully specified for Section D so no polling, retry, or backoff behavior is implemented in the wrong component? [Completeness, Spec §Section D]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “next upcoming course” defined precisely enough to remove ambiguity around null dates, past courses, and simultaneous start dates? [Clarity, Spec §Section A]
- [x] CHK007 Are the sorting requirements for participants clear enough to produce one deterministic order when names are partially missing or differ only by case and whitespace? [Clarity, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK008 Is the slide preview requirement specific enough to define which file types must be previewable in the Modal and what “displaying the slide content” means for non-HTML assets? [Ambiguity, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK009 Is the camera auto-refresh contract quantified clearly enough to distinguish initial load, post-success reset, additive backoff increments, cap behavior, and resume-on-focus behavior? [Clarity, Spec §Section D]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK010 Are the Section C status labels, state names, and supporting details consistent between the spec, component contract, and shared `EndpointResult` model? [Consistency, Spec §Section C]
- [x] CHK011 Are the empty-state copy requirements consistent across the spec and contracts for participants, slides, courses, and material status? [Consistency, Spec §Section A, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK012 Do the accessibility requirements for interactive elements align with the component-specific keyboard interaction requirements documented for participant expansion? [Consistency, Spec §Accessibility Requirements, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK013 Are the page-level responsive layout rules consistent with the section-level grid definitions so mobile and desktop behavior cannot conflict? [Consistency, Spec §Page Size Settings, Spec §Section Layout Rules]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK014 Are the acceptance criteria measurable enough to verify “render without errors” objectively across desktop and mobile viewports? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Acceptance Criteria]
- [x] CHK015 Are all required `data-testid` values specified with sufficient precision to avoid ambiguity about which element must carry each attribute? [Measurability, Spec §Required data-testid Attributes]
- [x] CHK016 Are the performance goals framed with clear measurement conditions, tooling assumptions, and target scenarios so they can be verified consistently? [Measurability, Spec §Performance Goals]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are requirements defined for the full state mapping of slide data across loading, ready, empty, and error scenarios in both Section A and Section B without duplication gaps? [Coverage, Spec §Section A, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK018 Are requirements defined for alternate participant data scenarios, including null names, missing detail fields, and zero participants? [Coverage, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK019 Are exception-flow requirements defined for endpoint probes that redirect, return unsupported `HEAD`, return `5xx`, or fail at the network layer? [Coverage, Spec §Section C]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery-flow requirements defined for the camera after transient failures, including how success resets backoff and how polling resumes after tab visibility changes? [Coverage, Spec §Section D]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Does the spec define what should happen when a slide file exists in `fetchSlideStatus().files` but cannot be previewed because its format is unsupported rather than missing? [Gap, Spec §Section B]
- [x] CHK022 Are date-display requirements defined for courses whose `startDate` or `endDate` is null or malformed, or is that case intentionally excluded? [Gap, Spec §Section A]
- [x] CHK023 Does the spec define fallback behavior when repeated camera retries continue indefinitely at the capped interval for a long-running outage? [Edge Case, Spec §Section D]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK024 Are keyboard accessibility requirements complete for every interactive control mentioned in the spec, including retry buttons, slide preview triggers, expand/collapse rows, and generate actions? [Completeness, Spec §Accessibility Requirements]
- [x] CHK025 Are color-contrast requirements traceable to all status chips, badges, and placeholder states that communicate meaning visually? [Coverage, Spec §Accessibility Requirements, Gap]
- [x] CHK026 Are no-CLS requirements specific enough to cover skeleton-to-content transitions in Section A and the aspect-ratio-preserving camera placeholder in Section D? [Clarity, Spec §Performance Goals, Spec §Section D]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK027 Are external dependency assumptions documented for Hemera API availability, local filesystem access, and snapshot endpoint behavior, including how each dependency failure maps to UI requirements? [Dependencies, Spec §Section A, Spec §Section B, Spec §Section D]
- [x] CHK028 Is it explicit whether logging requirements mean console logging, structured server logging, or monitoring integration for the various error states? [Assumption, Spec §Section A, Spec §Section B, Spec §Section D]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK029 Is there any unresolved conflict between the requirement for SSR-oriented performance goals and the client-owned polling/probe behavior in Sections C and D? [Conflict, Spec §Performance Goals, Spec §Section C, Spec §Section D]
- [x] CHK030 Is the optional CTA in the course empty state sufficiently constrained, or does it leave multiple acceptable implementations without a selection rule? [Ambiguity, Spec §Section A]
